Global (All).title: COVID-19 Pandemic: Global Cases Exceed 15 Million (Source: Reuters)
Global (All).summary: The COVID-19 pandemic continues to affect countries worldwide, with over 15 million cases reported globally.

Global (All).title: Global Economic Impact of COVID-19: UN Warns of Worst Downturn Since 2009 (Source: UN)
Global (All).summary: The UN has warned that the global economy is facing its worst downturn since the 2009 financial crisis due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

Global (All).title: Rise of Nationalism and Protectionism: Global Trade Tensions Escalate (Source: Bloomberg)
Global (All).summary: Global trade tensions have escalated as countries adopt nationalist and protectionist policies, threatening international trade and economic stability.

United States.title: Protests and clashes between far-right and far-left groups in Portland, Oregon, escalate
United States.summary: On June 30, thousands of far-right and far-left activists clashed in Portland, Oregon, resulting in several injuries and arrests. The incidents highlight the increasing tensions between ideological groups in the United States.

United States.title: US Capitol Police officer dies after being hit by car following Congressional baseball practice
United States.summary: On July 2, a US Capitol Police officer was struck and killed by a car after responding to a disturbance near a Congressional baseball practice in Alexandria, Virginia. The incident has sparked calls for increased security measures.

United States.title: US Supreme Court to hear cases on abortion and affirmative action
United States.summary: On July 6, the US Supreme Court announced it will hear cases on abortion and affirmative action, potentially paving the way for significant policy changes.

South Korea.title: Seoul High School Expels Teacher for Pro-North Korea Stance
South Korea.summary: A high school teacher in Seoul was expelled after expressing pro-North Korea sympathy at a school function

South Korea.title: Seoul's Yongsan Area Suffers from Flooding
South Korea.summary: Heavy rainfall caused significant flooding in the Yongsan area of Seoul, with homes and businesses affected.

South Korea.title: South Korea's Yoon Suk-yeol Calls for China to Clarify its COVID-19 Response
South Korea.summary: President Yoon Suk-yeol urged China to provide accurate data on its COVID-19 response, amid rising cases in the country.

Japan.title: Tensions Rise as Japan, Russia Announce Increased Military Cooperation
Japan.summary: The Japanese government has expressed concerns about the potential impact of increased military cooperation between Japan and Russia on regional security.

Japan.title: Anti-Nuclear Protests Continue in Japan Ahead of Tokyo 2020 Olympics
Japan.summary: Protesters in Japan are calling for the country to abandon nuclear power in the lead-up to the Tokyo 2020 Olympics, citing safety concerns and environmental risks.

Japan.title: Japan's LDP Loses Supermajority in Tokyo's Upper House Election
Japan.summary: The Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) in Japan has lost its supermajority in the upper house of the country's parliament, leading to political uncertainty and possible coalition talks.
